definition: Mind as a cognitive field or faculty, especially as the sixth internal sense field and in body-speech-mind formulations.
notes: The project keeps mind as the default because it is the cleanest general English, but the entry must not drift into treating mano as interchangeable with citta or viññāṇa. In the sixfold system mano is the internal mental field or faculty correlated with dhammas and mano-viññāṇa. In ethical triads it is the mental side of bodily, verbal, and mental action. Mental faculty can be used when that role needs to stay explicit. Translators should keep the faculty distinction audible in six-sense-field contexts and should not flatten mano into a generic consciousness term.

context_rules.context: In general doctrinal prose and in body-speech-mind expressions
In six-sense-field analysis where mano functions alongside eye, ear, nose, tongue, and body
context_rules.notes: Use when the faculty-role should remain audible in English.
Keep continuity with body, speech, and mind formulas.

context_rules.context: When the passage distinguishes mano from citta or viññāṇa
context_rules.notes: Use the more specific alternate rather than flattening the distinction.

example_phrases.pali: mano ca paṭicca dhamme ca uppajjati manoviññāṇaṃ
example_phrases.translation: dependent on the mental faculty and dhammas, mind-knowing arises

version_notes: Expanded to distinguish mano from citta and viññāṇa and to encode both six-sense-field and ethical-triad usage.
